summary: Decompiled a large portion of the Item Switch menu module, adding concrete implementations and prototypes for mnitemsw functions that manage item selection, item frequency, cursor/highlight animation, text creation/destruction, menu transitions, and GObj/JObj setup. The decomp.dev bot reported 3 new full matches totaling 1272 bytes and 8 additional near-matches/improvements in main/melee/mn/mnitemsw.
what_changed: Added many missing includes needed by mnitemsw.c, including baselib GObj/JObj/SisLib helpers, sysdolphin memory/debug/userdata headers, game-mode headers, language/card-game helpers, mnmainrule, and sc/types.
Introduced extern references for StaticModelDesc MenMainCursorIs_Top, StaticModelDesc MenMainConIs_Top, HSD_GObj* mnItemSw_804D6BE8, and u8 mn_804D6BB5.
Added local struct MnItemSwTable with animation/frame tables, item icon values, and item_order mapping, backed by static data mnItemSw_803ED340.
Added static f32 mnItemSw_804D4BA0[2] = { 0.0f, 1.0f } for item on/off animation values.

Implemented mnItemSw_80233A98 with a language-sensitive mapping for item IDs 17 and 19, returning alternate values 0x28 and 0x27 outside JP settings; this function was fully matched according to the bot report.

Implemented mnItemSw_80233B68 to update mn_804A04F0.hovered_selection and confirmed_selection from directional input bits, including wraparound between item columns and the special 0x1F/0x20 frequency/all-item selections; this function was fully matched.

Implemented fn_80233E10 as the Item Switch input process, reading menu inputs, handling Back/A/Start behavior, saving item settings through mn_8022E978, updating gmMainLib_8015CC58()->item_freq, triggering sound effects, and transitioning/destroying menu objects.
Implemented mnItemSw_80234104 to initialize item list visuals, update item icon animations, hide/show cursor objects, position the cursor based on column and y spacing, and clear relevant JObj flags.

Implemented mnItemSw_8023453C to update visuals when hovered or confirmed selection changes, including item highlight animations, item on/off animation, all/frequency animation, cursor position, and animation loop settings.

Implemented fn_80234C24 as the menu update/state routine, including enter/exit animation state handling, text allocation through HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC, language-sensitive text selection, object cleanup through HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4, and propagation of changed cursor/selection state into MnItemSwData.

Implemented mnItemSw_80235020 to construct an individual item JObj using MenMainCursorIs_Top, attach animations, set icon frame via mnItemSw_80233A98, set on/off state using mnItemSw_804D4BA0, and set or hide highlight visuals; this function was fully matched.

Implemented mnItemSw_802351A0 to create the Item Switch GObj, load MenMainConIs_Top, set GX/proc callbacks, allocate and initialize MnItemSwData, populate current item settings from gm_8016403C, attach item child JObjs into two columns, position cursor, initialize all-item/frequency animation, and return the new GObj.

Updated mnitemsw.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures for mnItemSw_80233B68, mnItemSw_80234104, mnItemSw_8023453C, fn_80234C24, mnItemSw_80235020, and mnItemSw_802351A0, and exported mnItemSw_804D6BE8.
smart_moves: Centralized magic animation/item data in a named local table struct, which made repeated JObj animation calls use the same data layout and helped expose .data matching progress.
Used explicit u8/u16 casts around hovered_selection and confirmed_selection, matching the menu state storage behavior and preserving special sentinel selections 0x1F and 0x20.
Kept mnItemSw_80233A98 under #pragma dont_inline, likely to preserve function boundary/codegen needed for its reported full match.
Modeled item list layout as two columns split at index 0x10, with y spacing derived from existing JObj translations instead of hardcoding every position.
Reused helper mnItemSw_8023405C to find per-item JObjs, reducing repeated traversal logic in visual update functions.
Stored and compared previous hovered/confirmed state before calling movement logic so sound effects only play when input actually changes selection state.
Preserved language-dependent item/icon/text behavior by checking lbLang_IsSettingJP and lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS rather than assuming a single regional asset mapping.
Converted header prototypes to concrete argument and return types once implementations revealed the data flow, improving future callsite decompilation.
decomp_lessons: For menu modules, first identify the persistent user_data struct and global menu state fields; most functions become state synchronization between globals, user_data, and JObj visuals.

Special menu selections often live outside the ordinary item index range. Here 0x1F and 0x20 are treated as sentinel cursor positions for non-item controls and require separate confirmed_selection handling.
Regional language differences can affect animation/icon indices, not just strings; keep small mapping helpers isolated when matching item menu code.
Menu visual setup can often be reconstructed from StaticModelDesc assets plus child indices found through lb_80011E24.
Animation tables with x00/x30-style unknown field names are still useful when grouped into a struct with documented offsets; naming can be refined later without losing matching progress.
When the data model is only partially understood, concrete function signatures in headers are still valuable if grounded in observed callsites and user_data usage.
For HSD menu code, toggling JObj flags, requesting specific animation frames, and immediately calling HSD_JObjAnim/AnimAll is a common matching pattern.
Use HSD_MemAlloc plus HSD_ASSERTREPORT and GObj_InitUserData when reconstructing GObj-owned menu data lifetime.
naming_conventions: Module-local symbols use the existing address-based mnItemSw_802xxxxx and mnItemSw_803/804xxxxx naming style.
Unidentified static data was grouped as struct MnItemSwTable with partially descriptive fields items and item_order while retaining offset-like names x00 and x30 for unknown animation arrays.
Temporary or not-yet-understood struct fields in MnItemSwData remain x21 and x23 rather than being overnamed.

The implementation uses descriptive locals such as hovered, buttons, old_hovered, old_confirmed, y_spacing, new_cursor, old_cursor, all_jobj, item_jobj, menu_changed, hovered_changed, and confirmed_changed where semantics are clear.
Function names fn_80233E10 and fn_80234C24 remain generic where no better symbol name was evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline was applied around mnItemSw_80233A98 to preserve matching behavior.
PAD_STACK(0x20) and PAD_STACK(0x18) were used in functions with stack-sensitive codegen.
Narrow casts to u8 and u16 were used heavily around global selection fields to match comparisons, wraparound, and array indexing.
The code uses small scoped blocks to control temporary lifetimes and stack/register allocation in larger functions.
Conditionals preserve explicit sentinel checks for cursor values 0x1F and 0x20 and range checks such as (u8)(sel - 0x1F) <= 1U.
Animation updates follow the HSD pattern HSD_JObjReqAnim(All), optional mn_8022F3D8/mn_8022ED6C, then HSD_JObjAnim(All).

The decomp.dev report shows full matches for mnItemSw_80233A98, mnItemSw_80233B68, and mnItemSw_80235020, and near-matches for mnItemSw_802351A0, mnItemSw_8023453C, mnItemSw_80234104, fn_80234C24, and fn_80233E10.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report: matched code increased to 62.21% (+0.03%, +1272 bytes), with 3 new matches and 8 improvements in unmatched mnitemsw items.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that refine names for MnItemSwData fields x21 and x23 or MnItemSwTable fields x00 and x30.
Search for later improvements to near-matched mnitemsw functions mnItemSw_802351A0, mnItemSw_8023453C, mnItemSw_80234104, fn_80234C24, and fn_80233E10.
Check related menu modules for reusable patterns around StaticModelDesc, HSD_SisLib text lifecycle, and two-column cursor positioning.
Investigate whether mnItemSw_804D6BE8 extern placement was later moved to a broader header or renamed.
